channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk and conservative influencer Ashley St. Clair publicly feuded on X on Monday after she accused the world’s richest man of withdrawing childcare support and refusing to take a paternity test. Musk defended himself against those claims, saying he has given St.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: New Python lock file format will specify dependencies
item.description: Python’s builders have accepted a proposal to create a universal lock file format for Python projects that would specify dependencies, enabling installation reproducibility in a Python environment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Worried about EV longevity? These 5 electric cars have all done over 200,000km on their original battery
item.description: A Hyundai Ioniq 5 owner recently clocked up 360,000 miles on the original battery but it isn't the only hyper-mileage EV.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Mortgage Giant Rocket Acquires Mr. Cooper in $9.4 Billion Deal
item.description: Rocket Cos. buys Mr. Cooper Group Inc. in all-stock deal, creating a business representing one in six mortgages in the US.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Global server sales almost doubled in Q4 with annual run rate likely to smash $300 billion in 2025
item.description: Server market surges as global sales nearly double in Q4 2024, pushing annual revenue toward a staggering $300 billion milestone by 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Love the MacBook Pro? A new model could be on the way, with some significant upgrades
item.description: A new leak claims that 2026 will finally deliver an OLED MacBook Pro, with a new M6 chip and thinner chassis.
item.dc:creator: christian.guyton@futurenet.com (Christian Guyton), TechRadar

item.title: New phishing attack uses real-time interception to bypass 2FA
item.description: This phishing kit bypasses 2FA via session hijacking and real-time credential theft. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from Astaroth phishing attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: DISA’s Jeff Marshall on aligning tech to solving warfighter problems
item.description: DISA pursues a two-pronged customer experience strategy to ensure its IT services meet warfighters’ needs, the director of J9 Hosting and Compute says.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: La guerra cultural de la IA: los sesgos ocultos en los modelos de entrenamiento pueden impulsar la propaganda política
item.description: El lanzamiento del DeepSeek, de fabricación china, ha generado un acalorado debate, aunque los críticos han ignorado en gran medida un enorme problema: la posibilidad de que China utilice un modelo de IA de este tipo para impulsar una agenda cultural y política en el resto del mundo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Terraform is evolving infrastructure as code
item.description: Last week I talked up OpenTofu—and for good reason. The Terraform fork has moved beyond being the darling of the infrastructure-as-code (IaC) community to becoming a real, if early, enterprise contender.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Worldwide spending on genAI to surge by hundreds of billions of dollars
item.description: CIOs should prepare for a 76.4% rise in global generative AI (genAI) spending this year, driven by improved foundational models and growing demand, according to a Gartner research report released today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump calls 'never ending' Signal leak story 'old and boring,' blasts media
item.description: President Trump slammed the media for its “never-ending” coverage of the Signal group chat leak, calling it “old and boring.” In a post Sunday on Truth Social, Trump praised Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: How to bring Apple’s ‘Hide My Email’ privacy to Android and Windows
item.description: Have you ever wanted to sign up for an online service but you didn’t want to provide your real email address as part of the process? There’s a good chance your email address has your actual name in it. Or perhaps you want to avoid the risk of getting spammed.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: 3 great, free Word alternatives in the wake of the Microsoft 365 price hike
item.description: Did everyone get the Microsoft 365 rate-hike notice? The personal plan is going from $70 a year to $100 a year.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: iMessage still lags behind its peers. 4 ways Apple should update it for iOS 19
item.description: Few apps are as inextricably linked to the iPhone as Apple’s Messages. Introduced with the original iPhone almost 18 years ago, the app (then called “Text”) has become the primary messenger for most iPhone users worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: GenAI, the future of fraud and why you may be an easy target
item.description: With the help of artificial intelligence, sophisticated fraud techniques emerging today are virtually undetectable to the untrained eye.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Nvidia chip shortage is coming, warns major Chinese server maker
item.description: One of China’s largest server makers, H3C, has flagged potential shortages of Nvidia’s H20 chip, the most advanced AI processor legally available domestically under U.S. export controls, in a client notice seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Tricentis’ Benjamin Baldi on revving up modernization with automated software testing
item.description: For Defense agencies, automation of enterprise software testing can help speed projects to completion. Tricentis’ Benjamin Baldi explains how.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Nikola founder says he was pardoned by Trump
item.description: Nikola founder Trevor Milton said Thursday that President Trump pardoned him for his 2022 conviction of misleading investors about the electric vehicle maker's technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google Cloud partners with mLogica to offer mainframe modernization
item.description: Google Cloud has partnered with database and mainframe modernization software provider mLogica to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications. The partnership will see Google Cloud combine its Dual Run service with mLogica’s LIBER*M automated code refactoring platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tiny electric vehicle takes half a parking space
item.description: Toyota's compact electric FT-Me is designed to tackle challenges of city living while offering an accessible and eco-friendly transportation solution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4
